title: Schradera clusiifolia
intro: Schradera clusiifolia, also known as Clusioides or Wild Forestiera, is a tropical plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Schradera clusiifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, such as isochlorogenic acid, isorhapontigenin, and epicatechin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Schradera clusiifolia is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, particularly those related to the respiratory and digestive systems.
taxonomy: Schradera clusiifolia is classified within the family Rubiaceae, a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is further divided into numerous genera, with Schradera being one of them.
